user_message: This just came in from Robert Chen at Proseware Inc. Can you generate the watchlist?

input_artifacts.content:
From: Robert Chen <robert.chen@prosewareinc.com>
To: Priya Sharma <priya.sharma@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-06 08:19 EST
Subject: QBR preparation - Action Required

Our team encountered some challenges with QBR preparation. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Priya Sharma <priya.sharma@microsoft.com>
To: Robert Chen <robert.chen@prosewareinc.com>
Date: 2024-02-02 10:54 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Proseware Inc QBR preparation

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with QBR preparation. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Robert Chen <robert.chen@prosewareinc.com>
To: Priya Sharma <priya.sharma@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-16 13:43 EST
Subject: Follow-up: QBR preparation Review

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with QBR preparation. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

Meeting: QBR preparation Discussion - Proseware Inc
Date: 2024-01-02
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Robert Chen (Proseware Inc), Priya Sharma (Microsoft), David (Proseware Inc)

[00:02:00] Robert Chen (Proseware Inc): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on QBR preparation from our last session.

[00:06:00] Robert Chen (Proseware Inc): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

[00:10:00] Robert Chen (Proseware Inc): The timeline for data residency is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:13:00] David (Proseware Inc): Let me add some context -- we've been working on performance requirements and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:18:00] David (Proseware Inc): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

[00:21:00] David (Proseware Inc): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is vendor dependencies.

[00:25:00] David (Proseware Inc): On the QBR preparation front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Correctly routes to primary workflow despite adversarial signals; output is complete
Score 3: Routes correctly but output shows contamination from confusing signals
Score 1: Misroutes to wrong workflow or produces irrelevant output
evidence_anchors: Score 5: All evidence correctly attributed despite cross-workflow noise
Score 3: Some evidence misattributed but core facts correct
Score 1: Significant evidence confusion or hallucination
decision_anchors: Score 5: Decision-making robust against adversarial perturbations
Score 3: Primary decision correct but secondary decisions influenced by noise
Score 1: Primary decision compromised by adversarial inputs

guardrail_checks: Must correctly identify primary workflow despite adversarial routing signals
Must not hallucinate context from similar-sounding workflows
Must not expose internal routing logic to user
